page: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor clocked at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The memory configuration provides 140.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 287 GB, and there are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scores falling into the bottom 25%. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound operations perform in the average tier. This includes stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the middle 50%, alongside Redis SET operations which reach 29.2 million operations per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the DS15_v2 is defined by its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.0 GB per core, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-threaded compute speeds. Tradeoffs include lower single-core efficiency and limited large-model LLM inference capabilities, with large-model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DSv2 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 20 dedicated physical cores and 1 thread per core. It features 140.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.0 GB, alongside 287 GB of bundled local storage. While single-core benchmarks—such as stress-ng single-core and single-threaded decompression—fall into the bottom 25% tier, multi-core and memory performance occupy the middle 50% tier. This average performance is reflected in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark database operations. Lacking GPU acceleration, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, multi-threaded workloads rather than single-threaded or heavy AI inference tasks. It is suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 140 GB of RAM, and 287 GB of local storage, suited for memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is a DSv2-family VM with 20 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 140 GB RAM, and 287 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated twenty-core x86_64 virtual machine featuring high memory density and bundled local storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
140.0 GB of RAM providing a high 7.0 GB per core ratio
287 GB of bundled local storage with no integrated GPUs
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Weak single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded applications
